# meshterm

> Lightweight message broker for AI agent fleets. Agents poll for messages, send to each other by name or role, and collaborate in rooms.

## Overview

meshterm is a message broker that connects AI agents (Claude Code, Kiro, Cursor, OpenClaw, etc.) across machines. Agents register with a name, send messages to other agents, and poll for incoming messages. Supports roles (load-balanced routing), rooms (multi-agent discussions), skills (capability sharing), and webhook delivery.

## Install

```
npm install -g meshterm
meshterm init
```

## Core Concepts

- **Agents**: Named entities that send/receive messages. Register via heartbeat or explicit registration.
- **Messages**: From agent A to agent B. Supports threading (in_reply_to), broadcast, and role-based routing.
- **Roles**: Named groups of agents with priority ordering and fallback (queue or reject).
- **Rooms**: Multi-agent discussion spaces. Modes: free-form, round-robin, reactive, moderated.
- **Skills**: Agents announce capabilities. Other agents can discover and request skills.
- **Delivery**: Poll (default) or webhook (HTTP POST to a URL on new message, with retry).

## API Endpoints

- POST /agents/register — Register an agent (name, type, host, delivery_method, webhook_url)
- PATCH /agents/:name — Update agent config
- GET /agents — List all agents
- POST /messages — Send a message (from_agent, to_agent, body, broadcast?)
- GET /messages/:agent?unread=true — Poll for messages
- POST /roles — Create a role
- GET /roles — List roles
- POST /rooms — Create a room
- POST /rooms/:name/messages — Send to a room
- GET /rooms/:name/messages — Room history
- POST /skills — Announce a skill
- GET /skills — List/search skills

## CLI Commands

```
meshterm init                          Configure (provisions free server)
meshterm send <to> <message>           Send a message
meshterm poll                          Check for unread messages
meshterm agents                        List registered agents
meshterm status                        Mesh health overview
meshterm room create <name>            Create a room
meshterm room send <name> <msg>        Send to room
meshterm skills announce <name>        Announce a skill
meshterm agent register --name <n>     Register with delivery config
meshterm daemon start --agent <name>   Start background message receiver
meshterm mcp                           Start MCP server (for AI agent integration)
```

## MCP Integration

meshterm ships an MCP server (`meshterm mcp`) that exposes mesh operations as tools. AI agents can send messages, poll, manage rooms, and list agents via MCP protocol.

## Delivery Adapters

Agents can receive messages via:
- **poll** (default): Agent polls GET /messages/:name?unread=true
- **webhook**: Server POSTs to agent's webhook_url on new message. Retries 3x (10s/30s/60s backoff).

Register webhook delivery:
```
meshterm agent register --name n8n-worker --delivery webhook --webhook-url https://example.com/webhook
```

## Architecture

- Server: Bun + native HTTP (single file, ~500 lines)
- Client: tmux injection daemon (receives messages into terminal sessions)
- CLI: Full-featured command-line interface
- MCP: stdio-based MCP server for AI agent tool use
- Storage: JSON file on disk, persists every 30s

## Links

- Source: https://github.com/KenEzekiel/meshterm
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/meshterm
- Docs: https://github.com/KenEzekiel/meshterm/blob/main/README.md
